title: Doctor extension: filesystem-edit fallback check (synthesizes knowledge_promoted for orphan canonical files)
description: Add a new doctor check that detects canonical knowledge entries (under .fabric/knowledge/{team,personal}/<type>/<id>--<slug>.md) that lack a corresponding knowledge_promoted event in events.jsonl. For each orphan, write a synthesized knowledge_promoted event with reason='[synthesized] filesystem-edit-fallback' so subsequent ledger consumers see a complete promotion trail. The check is a 'fixable_error' surfaced by `fab doctor` and applied automatically by `fab doctor --fix`.

files.target: checks[] array + new createKnowledgePromotedSynthesizedCheck function + runDoctorFix branch
files.change: Add `createKnowledgePromotedSynthesizedCheck(projectRoot, eventLedger)` to the checks[] array (after line 297 alongside existing knowledge.* checks). The check scans .fabric/knowledge/{team,personal}/<type>/<id>--<slug>.md files, parses stable_id from filename (regex /^(K[TP]-[DPGMR]-\d{4})--/), cross-references against events.jsonl filtering for knowledge_promoted events with matching stable_id. For each orphan (file present, no promoted event) raise a fixable_error issue with code 'knowledge_promoted_synthesized_required' and remediation 'Run fab doctor --fix to synthesize knowledge_promoted events'. runDoctorFix branch: when fixable_errors contains this code, for each orphan call appendEventLedgerEvent({event_type:'knowledge_promoted', stable_id, timestamp:fileStats.mtime.toISOString(), reason:'[synthesized] filesystem-edit-fallback'}).

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts:285-345 to confirm the checks[] additive pattern and runDoctorFix handler shape

Implement createKnowledgePromotedSynthesizedCheck(projectRoot, eventLedger): walk .fabric/knowledge/{team,personal}/{decisions,pitfalls,guidelines,models,processes}/*.md (existsSync-guarded readdirs); for each .md file extract stable_id from filename via /^(K[TP]-[DPGMR]-\d{4})--/.exec(basename); collect set of canonical_ids[]. From eventLedger filter events where event_type==='knowledge_promoted' && stable_id is set; collect promoted_ids set. orphans = canonical_ids - promoted_ids. Build issue per orphan: {code:'knowledge_promoted_synthesized_required', severity:'fixable_error', stable_id, file:relativePath, message:`Canonical entry ${stable_id} has no knowledge_promoted event in ledger`, fix:'fab doctor --fix synthesizes knowledge_promoted with reason=[synthesized] filesystem-edit-fallback'}
Add new check to checks[] array immediately after createCounterDesyncCheck (line 298 area) — keep same shape (return {status, issues, ...})

Extend runDoctorFix: add new branch after existing event-ledger fix paths: `if (before.fixable_errors.some(i => i.code === 'knowledge_promoted_synthesized_required')) { for (const issue of before.fixable_errors.filter(i => i.code === 'knowledge_promoted_synthesized_required')) { const fileStats = await stat(join(projectRoot, issue.file)); await appendEventLedgerEvent(projectRoot, {event_type:'knowledge_promoted', stable_id:issue.stable_id, timestamp:fileStats.mtime.toISOString(), reason:'[synthesized] filesystem-edit-fallback'}); fixed.push(issue); } }`
Confirm event-ledger.ts knowledge_promoted schema accepts the synthesized event shape (stable_id optional but populated, timestamp ISO datetime, reason free string) — no schema change required
Update DoctorIssue['code'] union type to include 'knowledge_promoted_synthesized_required'
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test`; commit as 'feat(server): add filesystem-edit fallback doctor check (rc.3 TASK-005)'

reference.examples: Mirror createStableIdCollisionCheck / createCounterDesyncCheck shape; reuse readEventLedger pattern from existing checks

criteria: createKnowledgePromotedSynthesizedCheck registered in checks[] array (verified by `fab doctor` listing it in checks output)
Check correctly identifies orphan when fixture has canonical KT-D-0001--foo.md but no knowledge_promoted event for that id (verified by integration test seeding tmpdir + asserting issue raised)
Check correctly NOT raises when canonical entry has matching knowledge_promoted event (negative test)
runDoctorFix appends synthesized event with reason='[synthesized] filesystem-edit-fallback' (verified by reading events.jsonl after --fix)
Synthesized event timestamp matches file mtime (deterministic, replayable)
Existing 19 doctor checks unchanged — regression suite passes

rationale.chosen_approach: Additive doctor check + runDoctorFix branch; reason field carries '[synthesized]' prefix to avoid envelope schema change
alternatives_considered: Add `synthesized: boolean` field to knowledge_promoted schema — rejected: requires shared schema bump and migration; reason-prefix is sufficient for forensic discovery
Server-side watcher that auto-promotes on filesystem move — rejected: doctor is the user-controlled forensic tool; auto-watch creates surprise side effects
Run synthesis lazily during fab_review search — rejected: scattering doctor concerns across MCP tools breaks ownership
decision_factors: Doctor already owns filesystem-vs-ledger reconciliation (existing knowledge_dir_unindexed, stable_id_collision checks)
Reason-prefix is grep-able for forensic queries
Mtime as timestamp is deterministic; replays produce identical synthesized events
rationale.tradeoffs: Reason-field overload vs schema field — string-prefix conventions risk drift. Mitigated by adding constant SYNTHESIZED_PROMOTED_REASON = '[synthesized] filesystem-edit-fallback' to a shared constants module

manual_checks: Manually mv `.fabric/knowledge/pending/decisions/foo.md` to `.fabric/knowledge/team/decisions/KT-D-0099--foo.md` (without invoking fab_review approve); run `fab doctor` — verify orphan reported; run `fab doctor --fix` — verify events.jsonl gains synthesized knowledge_promoted
Verify subsequent `fab doctor` shows zero orphan issues (idempotent)
success_metrics: doctor.ts coverage stays >=90% for the new check function
Existing doctor.test.ts passes without modification (additive only)

risks.description: Stable_id parsed from filename diverges from frontmatter id (out-of-sync state); synthesized event uses filename id but frontmatter id may differ
risks.mitigation: Doctor uses filename id (filesystem source-of-truth); frontmatter mismatch is a separate check (existing rule-meta-builder anomaly detection); document the rule clearly
risks.fallback: If users hit this, ship a paired check `frontmatter_id_mismatch` in rc.4
